goal: Build a local-first scanned-book OCR/RAG lab pipeline in Python 3.11 with modular CLI and synthetic-safe repository defaults.
scope: Chapter-level PDF splitting from config ranges
Pluggable OCR backend interface with optional backends
OCR QA summaries in JSON and Markdown
Optional local embedding index and query path
outputs: Page renders and optional contact sheet
constraints: No heavy processing in tests/scaffold task
Repo publish-safe: no raw data artifacts committed
Target resource policy: avoid intentional >40% RAM/VRAM
decisions: Argparse-based CLI to minimize runtime dependencies
Backends raise MissingDependencyError when optional tools are absent
Rule-based first-pass case extraction with no medical inference
Indexing supports sentence-transformers optionally with FAISS/Chroma
checklist: Config, schema, docs, and README created
success_criteria: uv run scanbook --help shows all required subcommands
Synthetic fixtures run through split->qa->extract->index->query smoke flow
handoff_instructions: Copy configs/book.example.yaml to book-specific config and adjust ranges
Run chapter split and manual page inspection first
Compare OCR backends on sample chapters before full run
Use QA report to refine OCR profile/backends
Run extraction and local index generation in batches
Optionally hand off selected chapter outputs to Gemini CLI externally
